title: The Battle-Field.
content:
They dropped like flakes, they dropped like stars,
    Like petals from a rose,
    When suddenly across the June
    A wind with fingers goes.

    They perished in the seamless grass, --
    No eye could find the place;
    But God on his repealless list
    Can summon every face.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "The Battle-Field.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
